hard.next_step: 6/2/26 MQ Per Julio they are still reviewing submissions for down selection to shortlist. 4/28/26 MQ Per Julio, Antonino is traveling this week. Suggested reaching out 5/7 for update. 4/8/26 MQ RFP response submitted 3/31. Antonino and Julio are doing initial review of submitted proposals, specifically evaluating how each aligns with their medium-to-long term strategy. I am offering to provide business case to support their efforts. Antonino expects to have update on next steps the week of 4/20. 3/18/26 MQ Foundever has issued an RFP for CLM, Sourcing and Vendor Management and Onboarding. Responses are due by 3/31/26. Foundever embarked in a Finance Transformation Project selecting SAP S4/Hana Public Cloud Edition for consolidating all ERP core Finance functionality across the Globe including the 42 Countries where Foundever has operations. The Implementation Project started January 2026 and will be executed in waves during next 2 years. First GoLive is January 2027 with 2 Countries and during 2027 and 2028 the remaining Countries to complete the 42 countries by the end of 2028. As part of the scope, not all the P2P functionalities were considered and Foundever is exploring with potential recognized names on the P2P spectrum for completing those and selecting, ideally one partner, for covering all the missing functionalities. The intention is having an initial estimated cost of licensing and implementation by the end of March for presenting a Business Case to Executive Committee. Once approval obtained, refining proposals during April to June for awarding one or two Partners for covering the missing functionalities, starting the implementation during 2026 for GoLive January 2027 of the first 2 Countries. 3/5/26 MQ Foundever is standardizing/migrating all 42 sites to HANA/S4. Initial sites are US and Phillipines and are slated to go live 1/27. They will be using SAP for transactional P2P and have proposed to Coupa a 2yr. ramp down (Coupa is pushing back, renewal EOY). Looking to Zycus for CLM, eSourcing and Supplier Management with potential for additional footprint after Coupa ramp down. Wants us to go-live 1/27 on initial sites with other sites implemented concurrently w/ ERP roll out schedule. Want us to potentially replace existing Coupa P2P for 2 years if Coupa negotiations go south. Julio is sending 2yr. overall rollout scope. 3/2/26 MQ Scheduled meeting on 3/5/26 with Julio for update of project status. 2/19/26 MQ Working with Mariana and Julio to lock down date for group for Supplier Onboarding demonstration. 12/29/25 MQ Followed up with Julio. He is OOO until 1/2/25. 12/3/25 MQ Held scheduled process timing/alignment meeting with Julio. He wants preliminary cost estimates for S2P suite vs. Supplier Management (onboarding) and eSourcing modules. Julio will gather metrics for pricing. He is checking schedules to see if it's feasible to schedule the follow up Supplier Management demo in December or wait until January.
hard.ais_why:
• The November 13 2025 demo (63 minutes) with Julio Lozano and six Foundever stakeholders is the strongest piece of evidence. Julio's reaction is captured as "Very, very pleasant surprise" at the feature set, and Andreina called the demo "very thorough and very interesting". The buyer engaged on AI-driven negotiation parameters, asking transparency questions on AI discount recommendations.
• A captured pain point reads "Need transparency on AI-driven discount recommendations to avoid overpaying". That is the buyer actively evaluating AI behaviour, not just being pitched. Products include Agentic AI, Merlin and Merlin Intake; AI importance is 'Important'; and the RFP for CLM, Sourcing and Vendor Management was responded to on 3/31.
• Multiple Foundever attendees - Antonino, Julio, Mariana, Andreina, David, Sadoc and Ana - joined the AI demo and asked detailed AI-related questions on multi-parameter AI negotiation, smart onboarding agent preview, and autonomous negotiation with market benchmarks. Strong cross-functional buyer engagement on AI-specific capabilities underpins the Hungry call.

north_star_verdict.headline: Healthy, multi-threaded modular S2P tender with real two-way buyer engagement, but the 21 Aug 2026 close (46 days out) is not credible: Foundever is still down-selecting submissions as of 2 Jun 2026, with ExecComm approval, modular pricing and contracting all still ahead. The deal is live; the date is wrong.
north_star_verdict.math: Close 2026-08-21 = 46 days out. Buyer still 'reviewing submissions for down selection to shortlist' per Next Step 2026-06-02. Last SF activity 2026-06-23; genuine buyer email reply received 2026-06-01 (two-way engagement is real). RFP responses submitted 2026-03-31. Remaining gates (shortlist decision, modular pricing, ExecComm business case, MSA/SOW) realistically need 4-6 months, pushing close to Q4 2026. The true forcing function is the Jan 2027 US/Philippines S/4HANA go-live, not the Aug 2026 forecast date.
evidence: Next Step 2026-06-02: 'Per Julio they are still reviewing submissions for down selection to shortlist.'
Julio Lozano on 2026-03-05 process call: buyer's own timeline targets a Q2 2026 decision path and Jan 2027 go-live, not an Aug 2026 contract.

Buyer email reply received 2026-06-01 ('still in the process of assessing the options received as part of the RFI') confirms genuine two-way engagement within the last month, not rep activity into silence.
CloseDate slipped 2026-06-26 -> 2026-08-21 on 2026-06-02 (Marc Quessenberry), a rep-side placeholder push, not a buyer-committed date.

metrics.narrative: The buyer has a clear cost-driven rationale but no fully quantified Zycus business case yet. Julio Lozano framed it bluntly on 2026-03-05: 'It's gonna be the most optimized cost solution that we can try get at the very end,' with a worked example ('if we are paying now $100 for the full suite of Coupa, and we are paying $25... we are not interested in paying on top'), signalling they will pay only for the 3-4 modules they need. Julio confirmed by email on 2026-03-27 that Foundever's total 2025 spend is 'around $100M,' the sizing anchor for savings. The missing number is Zycus's own modular pricing plus a hard/soft-dollar ROI, which the buyer needs to build the ExecComm business case. Quantifying ROI vs Coupa's renewal cost is the open move.

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is the Foundever Executive Committee / Global CFO, mapped but not engaged. Julio Lozano explained the structure on 2026-03-05: 'we don't have a global head of procurement... for now it's a team on the procurement leadership' and 'our global CFO is supervising us in his very few minutes.' Julio's own next step (per Next_Step 2026-03-18) is to secure 'an initial estimated cost of licensing and implementation by the end of March for presenting a Business Case to the relevant stakeholder is the lead evaluator and process driver but not the signer; final award needs ExecComm approval. Because this is a champion-driven tender, the path to the CFO runs through Julio, so arm him to broker it rather than cold-outreach the CFO. Building that path is the highest-value gap after pricing.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are documented and cost-led. Julio Lozano stated the primary lens on 2026-03-05 ('It's gonna be the most optimized cost solution... we are gonna go with the cheapest one for sure') while cautioning it is not the sole criterion. Functional criteria surfaced across the 13 Nov 2025 demo: multi-currency and native-language sourcing for a 42-country footprint, custom-field flexibility (David Avila asked whether custom fields translate; John highlighted competitors cannot report on custom fields), autonomous AI negotiation with transparent discount logic (Julio pressed on how the AI derives the recommended discount, wary of leaving savings on the table), phased supplier onboarding gated by terms acceptance (Antonino Cappuccio), and bi-directional integration with SAP S/4HANA. The RFP for CLM, Sourcing, Vendor Management and Onboarding, responded 2026-03-31, is the formal criteria set.
sources: Avoma S2P Demonstration 2025-11-13 (Julio Lozano, David Avila, Antonino Cappuccio)

decision_process.narrative: Formal RFP run by Foundever procurement leadership. RFP issued ~18 Mar 2026, responses due 31 Mar 2026 and submitted on time. Per Next_Step 2026-04-08, Antonino Cappuccio and Julio Lozano did the initial review evaluating 'how each aligns with their medium-to-long term strategy'; as of 2026-06-02 they are still down-selecting to a shortlist. The approval chain (Julio, 2026-03-18): procurement leadership shortlists, an initial licensing plus implementation cost estimate goes to the Executive Committee for business-case approval, then proposals are refined April-June to award 'one or two partners.' The committee widened across NA/LATAM/APAC over the demo sequence. A final round of vendor presentations is expected before award (Marc's 2026-06-01 email: 'Will you be selecting a shortlist of vendors for a final round of presentations?').

paper_process.narrative: Paper mechanics are partly visible and not yet started. The hard contracting forcing function is the Coupa contract expiry at end of 2026 and the Jan 2027 US/Philippines S/4HANA go-live, so a signed solution is needed well before then (Julio, 2026-03-05). The bi-directional S/4HANA integration scope (two-way vendor-master validation and contract sync) must be defined before an SOW can be finalized. As new business this needs an MSA plus Order Form plus a separately-signed SOW; none has been drafted, no legal or InfoSec review has occurred, and no paper owner is named on either side. Julio also owes a 2-year overall rollout scope document he committed to send on 2026-03-05. Naming a paper owner and pre-staging MSA/SOW plus InfoSec sequencing should follow the shortlist decision.
sources: SF Next_Step 2026-03-05 (Coupa EOY expiry, Jan 2027 go-live)

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is clear and operationally specific. Foundever is migrating all 42 countries onto SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, which covers only transactional P2P and leaves eSourcing, vendor onboarding, vendor management and contracting uncovered, and the company has decided to exit Coupa (Julio, 2026-03-05: 'company decision is we need to move away from the Coupa platform'). Coupa is being ramped down to transactional-only as SAP absorbs that layer, but Foundever needs a solution for the four uncovered modules from the Jan 2027 go-live onward, ramping to all 42 countries by 2028. A live operational pain is global multi-currency and native-language sourcing across LATAM/APAC/Europe suppliers, which drove the 13 Nov 2025 translation demo. Pain owners: Julio Lozano (eSourcing, primary user) and Sadoc Medina (sourcing functionality), with Antonino Cappuccio owning the finance-transformation link.

champion.narrative: Julio Lozano (Global Category Manager IT) is the developing champion: he drives the selection committee, sourced Zycus into the RFP, proactively brokers internal alignment, and owns the eSourcing use case as a primary user (2026-03-05: 'I'm the one engaging with the candidates and taking a look of the commercials mostly... one of the primary users would be on my end on the eSourcing component'). He confirmed he drives the process when Marc asked directly on 2025-12-03 ('you're the main driver of this selection committee'). What is still missing is proven access to and advocacy with the economic buyer: he has not carried a priced business case to the ExecComm. Critically the rep has under-served him, the modular pricing he needs has been outstanding since 2025-12-03. Equip Julio with assumption-based modular pricing plus a one-page ROI deck he can carry upward to convert him from developing to strong.

competition.narrative: Coupa is the incumbent and the live competitive reference: used today for sourcing, contracting (metadata only), onboarding and transactional P2P, now in a 2-year ramp-down negotiation with a contract expiring end of 2026. Julio Lozano noted on 2026-03-05 that Coupa is 'not happy to see that we are in a wrap up' and is 'really interested in offering only the modules... for them it's just the bundle.' That commercial friction is Zycus's wedge: Foundever explicitly wants to pay only for the 3-4 modules S/4HANA does not cover, and Coupa's bundle-only posture is misaligned with a cost-cutting buyer. Other unnamed RFP respondents are in the down-select ('the 3 options that we are evaluating') but no evidence ranks them as a serious threat. Risk: if Coupa concedes an acceptable modular ramp-down, incumbent familiarity makes them the 'safe' option, which is exactly why getting our priced, integration-validated case in front of the committee first matters.

deal_movement.summary: Live but slow: RFP responses submitted 2026-03-31; buyer is still down-selecting to a shortlist as of 2026-06-02; genuine two-way email engagement as recent as 2026-06-01, last SF activity 2026-06-23. Close date slipped 26 Jun -> 21 Aug on 2026-06-02 (a rep placeholder push, not a buyer-committed date).

items.change: Amount raised 250000 -> 279300 and forecast Pipeline -> Best Case
RFP response for CLM, Sourcing and Vendor Management submitted
Buyer confirmed still assessing RFI/RFP options (email reply)

competitive_position.summary: Coupa is the dominant current threat as the incumbent in active renewal negotiation: displaced for transactional P2P by SAP S/4HANA but in play for the four uncovered modules. As of 2026-03-05 Coupa is resisting the ramp-down and pushing a full bundle that conflicts with Foundever's cost-optimized modular intent, and that misalignment is Zycus's primary wedge. Other unnamed RFP respondents are in the down-select but no evidence ranks them as a serious threat.

competitors.how_we_win: Match the cost-optimized modular scope Coupa refuses to unbundle, prove validated bi-directional S/4HANA integration, and land a lower total cost than Coupa's full-suite renewal.

competitors.name: Other RFP respondents (unnamed)
competitors.quote: we have the opportunity to see more than the one we need to consider... the 3 options that we are evaluating.
competitors.how_we_win: Be first to deliver modular pricing and an S/4HANA integration plan so Foundever can build the ExecComm business case around Zycus.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus lands strongly on functional fit (multi-currency/language sourcing, AI negotiation, custom-field flexibility) but is exposed on modular pricing and unproven on the bi-directional S/4HANA integration the buyer requires.

it combines your target price and market intelligence... it will check online on Amazon as well as other places to do a benchmark.

best_practice_check.summary: Win-strategy levers for a champion-driven, cost-led RFP with an unengaged economic buyer and a resisting incumbent.
flags: Build the path to the Global ExecComm through Julio: no exec is engaged with the budget owner who must approve the award.
Get Zycus modular pricing plus an integration-validated ROI case in front of the committee before Coupa concedes a modular ramp-down.
Multi-thread to David Avila (finance, primary contact) to co-build the savings model, rather than routing everything through Julio alone.

champion_strength.summary: Julio Lozano is a developing champion: he drives the selection committee, sourced Zycus into the RFP, owns the eSourcing use case, and brokers internal alignment, but has not yet been equipped to carry a priced business case to the CFO/ExecComm and shows no proof of advocacy at the budget level.
champion_strength.source: Avoma Process Alignment Review 2025-12-03 / Project Status Update 2026-03-05 (Julio Lozano)

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI story landed with a cross-functional audience: the 13 Nov 2025 demo drew detailed buyer questions on autonomous negotiation transparency, multi-parameter AI discount logic and AI-assisted scoring. AIS is Moderate (7/10); the agentic/Merlin narrative is resonating but has not yet been converted into a quantified savings case.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 7 / Moderate is fair: Foundever actively evaluated AI behaviour (Julio pressed on how the AI derives recommended discounts, wary of leaving savings on the table; David Avila probed custom-field translation), showing genuine AI appetite rather than passive interest, but the primary buying driver is cost, not AI ambition. Products include Agentic AI, Merlin and Merlin Intake.

items.detail: Global Executive Committee, the award approver, has never engaged Zycus.
21 Aug 2026 close is indefensible against a still-open shortlist and a Jan 2027 go-live; realistic close is Q4 2026.
Zycus modular pricing/ROI not yet before the committee; Coupa could re-anchor as the safe option if it concedes a modular ramp-down.
Bi-directional SAP S/4HANA vendor-master integration scope is undefined and unproven.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live but cost-gated and slow; the buyer replies within days but the shortlist decision is overdue and the scope has narrowed defensively on cost grounds.
items: Buyer email reply received 2026-06-01 confirms two-way engagement within the last month.
Promised shortlist update (week of 2026-04-20) is overdue as of 2026-06-02.

scope_change.from: Full Source-to-Pay suite replacing Coupa across all functions
scope_change.to: 3-4 modules (eSourcing, supplier onboarding, vendor management, contracting) layered on SAP S/4HANA transactional P2P, phased across 42 countries 2026-2028
scope_change.detail: Cost-driven, defensive narrowing: Foundever selected SAP S/4HANA for transactional P2P and wants to pay only for the uncovered modules, not a full suite (Julio Lozano, 2026-03-05: 'we are not interested in paying on top... we only need some modules'), with a phased ramp rather than big-bang.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Sole-fit modular S2P play into a cost-driven SAP S/4HANA transformation: Julio Lozano sourced Zycus into the RFP and incumbent Coupa's bundle-only posture is misaligned, but no economic buyer is engaged, the buyer is still down-selecting three vendors as of 2 Jun 2026, and the 21 Aug close is not credible against a Jan 2027 go-live.

win_position.text: Formal Evaluation caps the win read: strong modular fit and a developing champion earn a moderate score, held down because no economic buyer is engaged and Foundever is still down-selecting three vendors as of 2 Jun 2026.
Coupa's bundle-only renewal posture conflicts with Foundever's cost-optimized modular intent, giving Zycus a clean wedge (Julio Lozano, Avoma 5 Mar 2026).
No Zycus modular pricing has reached the committee, the exact number Julio needs to build the ExecComm business case, outstanding since 3 Dec 2025.
The Global CFO / Executive Committee that must approve the award has never engaged Zycus (Julio Lozano, 5 Mar 2026).

deal_momentum.text: Engagement is genuinely current: buyer email reply received 1 Jun 2026 and last Salesforce activity 23 Jun 2026.
The buyer's promised shortlist update (week of 20 Apr 2026) is overdue and the close date was pushed from 26 Jun to 21 Aug on 2 Jun 2026.

customer_commitment.text: Six cross-functional Foundever stakeholders joined the 13 Nov 2025 AI demo, and Andreina Woodworth called it 'very thorough and very interesting'.
Commitment is cost-gated: Julio said Foundever will pick 'the most optimized cost solution' and are still evaluating three options (3 Dec 2025 / 5 Mar 2026).

deal_risk.text: The 21 Aug 2026 forecast is indefensible: shortlist decision, ExecComm approval, modular pricing and MSA/SOW all remain, realistically pushing close to Q4 2026.
Scope is defensively narrowed to 3-4 modules on cost grounds, and a Coupa modular ramp-down concession could re-anchor the incumbent as the safe option.

close_plan_concretizing.evidence: No mutual close plan; close date is a rep placeholder (26 Jun -> 21 Aug) not a buyer-committed date.

executive_alignment.evidence: Global CFO / ExecComm, the award approver, has never engaged Zycus (Julio Lozano, 5 Mar 2026).

customer_preference.evidence: Julio Lozano: 'your solution is really good... we know that you are offering a good solution for us' (3 Dec 2025), but explicitly cost-driven and still evaluating three options with no decisive lean yet.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative cost rationale and sizing (total 2025 spend ~$100M, Coupa full-suite vs modular framing) but no quantified Zycus ROI or modular pricing before the committee yet.

critical_signals.text: Incumbent Coupa is being ramped down and is resisting, pushing a full bundle while Foundever will only pay for the uncovered modules, our clearest wedge (5 Mar 2026).
We still cannot justify a price to the committee: our modular pricing and ROI case have not reached them, the number Julio needs to go to the CFO.
The Global CFO and Executive Committee own the award decision but have never engaged Zycus, and we only reach them through Julio.
The 13 Nov 2025 multi-currency AI demo landed well with six stakeholders (Andreina: 'very thorough and very interesting'), but the buyer has since gone quiet on next steps.
Buyer needs bi-directional SAP S/4HANA vendor-master integration before an SOW can close, and that scope is still undefined (5 Mar 2026).

expansion_context.note: No sibling Closed-Won on Foundever; this is a net-new modular S2P deal, so executive access must still be earned.

items.expected_effect: Stabilizes deal scope and pricing assumptions, protecting against a Coupa modular concession re-anchoring the incumbent.

items.action: Reconfirm the Jan 2027 go-live plan and align a realistic contracting timeline.
items.trigger: The Aug 2026 close is 5 months before the Jan 2027 US/Philippines go-live, an inconsistent sequence.

commentary.win_position: Win 34 is carried by momentum 64 vs stage-expected 52; preference strength +0.40 (weight 20). Offsetting it: scope narrowed vs prior — Cost-driven, defensive narrowing: Foundever selected SAP S/4HANA for transactional P2P and wants to pay only for….
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 64 (moving forward): Next Step carries 15 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 0: no break-risk was observed in the swept evidence. Coverage is thin, so read this as 'nothing seen yet,' not 'nothing there.'
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 27 is anchored on the stage win (34), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 64, and dragged by risk 0. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 2 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
